info.name: JADU - Full API
info.description:
**JADU** (v0.1.0) – Synthetic society simulation: survey orchestration, analytics, evaluation.

**Flow:** 1) POST /population/generate → 2) GET /agents (optional) → 3) POST /survey or POST /survey/multi → 4) GET /analytics/:survey_id, POST /evaluate/:survey_id. Simulation, discovery, calibration can be used as needed.

**Env:** base_url, session_id, survey_id, agent_id. See postman/README.md for full input/output and flow per API.

item.name: population
item.description: **Population** – Generate synthetic agents and social graph. Run **POST /population/generate** first before any survey, simulation, or analytics.

request.description:
**Purpose:** Creates synthetic population (personas), Barabási–Albert social graph, agent state.

**Flow:** Call once at session start; then use GET /agents, POST /survey, etc.

**Input (body):** n (int, 10–10000, default 500), method ("monte_carlo"|"bayesian"|"ipf"), id_prefix (string), seed (int, optional).

**Output (200):** n, method, realism_passed, realism_score, per_attribute, segment_distribution.

item.name: agents
item.description: **Agents** – List and inspect synthetic agents. Requires population from POST /population/generate.

request.description:
**Purpose:** Paginated list of agent summaries (demographics).

**Flow:** After POST /population/generate; use to get agent_id for GET /agents/:agent_id.

**Input (query):** location, nationality (optional filters), limit (1–1000, default 100), offset (default 0).

**Output (200):** Array of { agent_id, age, nationality, income, location, occupation }.

**Purpose:** Full record for one agent: persona + state.

**Flow:** Set path agent_id (e.g. from GET /agents or env).

**Input (path):** agent_id (string, required).

**Output (200):** agent_id, persona (object), state (object or null). 404 if not found.

item.name: survey
item.description: **Survey** – Single-question (sync) and multi-question (async) surveys. Copy survey_id from POST /survey for analytics and evaluation.

request.description:
**Purpose:** One question, full response set returned immediately (sync).

**Flow:** After population. Copy response survey_id to env for GET results, GET analytics, POST evaluate.

**Input (body):** question (required), question_id, use_archetypes (default false), options[], current_events[].

**Output (200):** survey_id, question, responses[{ agent_id, answer, sampled_option, distribution, demographics, lifestyle, error }], n_total.

**Purpose:** Retrieve stored results for a survey (same as POST /survey response).

**Flow:** Use survey_id from POST /survey (or env).

**Input (path):** survey_id (required).

**Output (200):** survey_id, question, responses, n_total. 404 if not found.

**Purpose:** Start multi-question survey in background; returns session_id.

**Flow:** 1) Call this → 2) Poll GET progress until status completed → 3) GET session results. Collection script sets session_id.

**Input (body):** questions[{ question, question_id, options? }], use_archetypes, social_influence_between_rounds, summarize_every (1–50).

**Output (200):** session_id, current_round, total_rounds, status (running|completed|failed), completed_questions[].

**Purpose:** Poll multi-survey progress.

**Flow:** After POST /survey/multi; poll until status is "completed" then GET results.

**Input (path):** session_id (from POST /survey/multi).

**Output (200):** session_id, current_round, total_rounds, status, completed_questions[].

**Purpose:** Full results for completed multi-survey.

**Flow:** Call when progress status is "completed".

**Input (path):** session_id.

**Output (200):** session_id, questions[], rounds[{ round_idx, question, question_id, responses, n_total, elapsed_seconds }], total_responses, elapsed_seconds, status. 409 if still running.

**Purpose:** Results for one round of a multi-survey.

**Flow:** After session completed; round_idx 0-based.

**Input (path):** session_id, round_idx (int).

**Output (200):** round_idx, question, question_id, responses[], n_total, elapsed_seconds.

item.name: simulation
item.description: **Simulation** – Run time steps, inject events, run scenarios (isolated or compared), causal graph and ATE. Requires population.

request.description:
**Purpose:** Run N days of simulation (events, social influence, state updates). Mutates global state.

**Flow:** After population; optionally POST /simulation/events first.

**Input (body):** days (int, 1–365, default 30).

**Output (200):** status, days, n_agents. 400 if no population.

**Purpose:** Schedule a world event for a future simulation run.

**Input (body):** day (≥0), type (price_change|policy|infrastructure|market|new_service|new_metro_station), payload (object), district (optional).

**Output (200):** status, event_type, day, pending_events.

**Purpose:** List pending events and global_params.

**Output (200):** pending_events[], global_params.

**Purpose:** Current status.

**Output (200):** population_size, social_graph_loaded, pending_events.

**Purpose:** Run scenario in isolation (no global state change).

**Input (body):** name, days, seed, events[{ day, type, payload, district }].

**Output (200):** name, days, seed, population_size, dimension_means.

**Purpose:** Run two scenarios, return diff of macro metrics.

**Input (body):** scenario_a, scenario_b (same shape as POST /scenario).

**Output (200):** Comparison object.

**Purpose:** Run scenario then survey post-scenario population.

**Input (body):** scenario, questions[] (min 1).

**Output (200):** name, days, population_size, dimension_means, belief_means, survey_results, timeline.

**Purpose:** Run two scenarios, survey both, return comparison.

**Input (body):** scenario_a, scenario_b, questions[].

**Purpose:** Default causal graph (nodes, edges).

**Output (200):** Graph structure.

**Purpose:** Counterfactual do-intervention.

**Input (body):** intervention (var→value), observational (optional).

**Output (200):** counterfactual_values.

**Purpose:** Estimate Average Treatment Effect.

**Input (body):** treatment, outcome (strings), confounders[], treatment_value, control_value.

**Output (200):** treatment, outcome, ate.

**Purpose:** Learn causal structure from timeline data.

**Input (body):** timeline (array).

**Output (200):** Learned graph.

item.name: analytics
item.description: **Analytics** – Segmented analytics and insights for a survey. Requires survey_id from a completed survey.

request.description:
**Purpose:** Segmented response distributions and insight text for a survey.

**Flow:** After POST /survey (or completed multi); set survey_id in path/env.

**Input (path):** survey_id. **Query:** segment_by (location|income|nationality|age, default location).

**Output (200):** survey_id, segment_by, aggregated (segment→{option→proportion}), insights[]. 404 if survey not found.

item.name: evaluation
item.description: **Evaluation** – Run realism, drift, consistency, similarity, optional LLM judge on survey responses. Requires population and survey with results.

request.description:
**Purpose:** Run evaluation framework on survey responses (realism, drift, consistency, distribution, narrative similarity, optional LLM judge).

**Flow:** After population + survey with results; set survey_id in path.

**Input (path):** survey_id. **Body:** run_judge, judge_sample, realism_threshold, drift_threshold, run_similarity, similarity_threshold.

**Output (200):** population_realism, drift, consistency_score, distribution_validation, narrative_similarity, llm_judge, dashboard, quantitative_metrics, summary. 400 if no population or survey not found.

**Purpose:** Retrieve stored evaluation report by ID (often same as survey_id).

**Input (path):** evaluation_id.

**Output (200):** Report object. 404 if not found.

item.name: discovery
item.description: **Discovery** – Domain auto-setup and dimension discovery from questions. No population required.

body.raw:
{
  "domain_name": "food_delivery",
  "description": "Food delivery and preferences",
  "sample_questions": ["How often do you order delivery?"],
  "city_name": "Dubai",
  "currency": "USD",
  "reference_data": null
}

request.description:
**Purpose:** Create domain config under data/domains/<domain_id>/.

**Input (body):** domain_name (required), description, sample_questions[], city_name, currency, reference_data.

**Output (200):** domain_id, message.

body.raw:
{
  "questions": ["How often do you use delivery?", "What influences your choice?"],
  "n_behavioral": 12,
  "n_belief": 7,
  "domain_id": null,
  "save": false
}

request.description:
**Purpose:** Discover behavioral and belief dimensions from questions (e.g. via LLM). Optionally save to domain_id.

**Input (body):** questions[] (min 1), n_behavioral (1–50), n_belief (1–30), domain_id, save (bool).

**Output (200):** behavioral[], belief[] (name, description, representative_questions), question_to_dimension, saved.

item.name: calibration
item.description: **Calibration** – Fit factor weights to reference distributions; upload real data to get reference distribution. Auto-weights and fit require population.

request.description:
**Purpose:** Optimize factor weights for multiple questions to match reference distributions.

**Flow:** Population required. Optionally get reference_distributions from POST /calibration/upload-data.

**Input (body):** questions[], reference_distributions (question→{option→proportion}), n_iterations (5–500), seed.

**Output (200):** overall_loss, results[{ question, learned_weights, best_loss, converged }]. 400 if no population.

**Purpose:** Calibrate one question to a reference distribution.

**Input (body):** question, reference_distribution (option→proportion), demographics_cols[], n_iterations (min 5).

**Output (200):** question, learned_weights, best_loss, converged, n_iterations. 400 if no population.

**Purpose:** Upload real survey data; returns computed reference distribution for use in fit/auto-weights.

**Input (body):** question, responses[] (required), demographics[] (optional).

**Output (200):** question, n_responses, reference_distribution.
